raw_response:
**Overall rating: TRUE**

GitHub’s documentation supports the core idea that the `/user/emails` endpoint shows email addresses and marks which one is public, and that the `visibility` field indicates whether an email is publicly visible rather than merely readable by the API user. The statement that only one email can have `visibility=public` is consistent with GitHub’s design for a single public email on a profile, though the docs more explicitly say you can set “your primary email” to be public than they spell out the “only one at a time” rule in those exact words.[1][2]

## Claim analysis

| Claim | Rating | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| “On a GitHub user account, only ONE email at a time can have `visibility=public` via the GitHub API's `/user/emails` endpoint.” | TRUE | GitHub says the endpoint lists all email addresses and “specifies which one is visible to the public,” and the public-visibility setting is applied to the primary email, implying a single public email at a time [1][2]. |
| “The `visibility` field marks which email is displayed as the public profile email on github.com, not whether the email is generally readable.” | TRUE | GitHub’s docs describe the `visibility`/public-email behavior as the publicly visible email address shown on the profile page, and distinguish this from simply listing email addresses via the authenticated endpoint [1][2]. |

## Source URLs

- GitHub REST API endpoints for emails: https://docs.github.com/en/rest/users/emails[1]
- GitHub REST API endpoints for users: https://docs.github.com/en/rest/users/users?apiVersion=2026-03-10[2]

## Short summary

The content is accurate: GitHub treats `visibility=public` as the single public profile email, not as a generic “readable by the API” flag.[2][1]
